pages.raw_text:
At Helena, Mont, Mrs Maggie
M. Brickell, formerly of Spokane.
sued the Northern Pacific for $40,-
600 damages for the death of her
husband, killed in smashup last
February.


The town of Lane, Idaho, will
hold its annual picnic and barbecue
next Saturday, when people are ex-
pected from all parts of the Occur
dAlenes.


Lewiston, Idaho, is putting down
the lid with a clang in the red
light district and many men and
women are being run out of town
by the present moral crusade of
the administration.


September Is is expected to see
the start of the machinery in the
big lumber mill of the Weyer-
haeusers at Potlatch. The new
town Is to have churches, a bank
and company store.


Grasshoppers have done consid-
cradle damage to grain in the
vicinity of Bonita in the Big Bend.
The crop Is found to be of inferior
grade and only half yield.


Kittitas county estimates place
the wheat crop at 425,000 bushels
and the hay at 10000 tons, the
value being, respectively, $235,O0O
and $4SO,O0O.


Hook Smith swept the state of
Georgia for governor in the demo-
cratic primaries. He will have 219
of total of 366 in the convention.


In the Wall street railroad boom
yesterday Great Northern stock
reached 327. Hartman holdings
were strong and steady.


The recount of Seattle ballots in
the last official report is expected
shortly. Sixty-six ballots have
been held out. six of which at least
Indicate fraud. Prosecuting Attor-
ney Macintosh is exhibiting some
interest in the matter.


The vexed question of whether
or not the lid will be clapped on
the city of Snohomish Sundays Is
to be settled by popular vote at
the coming election.
